title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to establish a center on artificial intelligence to ensure continued United States leadership in research, development, and evaluation of artificial intelligence systems, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This document proposes changes to the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act to create a new center focused on artificial intelligence research and development in the US. The aim is to maintain the country's leading position in AI systems research, evaluation, and development.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This act aims to regulate and govern the development and use of advanced technology and artificial intelligence in various sectors. The details of the act are unclear without a description, but it is likely to establish guidelines and oversight for AI-related initiatives.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act is a proposed law that aims to assess the risk of generative AI technology being used for terrorist activities. This law likely seeks to identify and mitigate the potential dangers posed by AI systems capable of generating malicious content.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 aims to prevent the misuse of AI in elections. This law is supposed to establish rules and guidelines to ensure the integrity and security of elections.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 aims to establish a framework for the regulation and governance of artificial intelligence across various sectors. This act likely provides guidelines and rules to balance AI development with safety, security, and ethical considerations.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act aims to prevent AI systems from censoring certain types of information. The policy seeks to ensure that AI systems do not unfairly restrict or block access to information.

title: To require disclosure when personalized algorithmic pricing is used, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy aims to mandate disclosure when companies use personalized algorithmic pricing, which means they charge different prices based on individual customers' data. The purpose of the policy is likely to increase transparency and fairness in business practices.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to establish guidelines and regulations for the development and deployment of artificial intelligence. However, the specifics of the act's intentions and provisions are unknown due to a lack of description.

title: To require reports regarding artificial intelligence-related job impacts, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This policy aims to require companies to report on job impacts caused by artificial intelligence. The policy also includes other purposes, but their specifics are not stated.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to restrict employers from using automated decision systems in specific ways. It's intended to address and regulate the impact of automation on employment.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill proposes to add a tax on AI systems that are deemed "systemically important", meaning they pose a significant risk to the economy if they fail. The purpose of the tax is to regulate and manage these high-stakes AI activities.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This document outlines a proposed law, the Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026, which likely aims to address the environmental consequences of artificial intelligence technology.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 594.
summary: This document outlines an act related to the Small Business Administration's use of artificial intelligence, likely aiming to establish guidelines or regulations for its utilization.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating the use of artificial intelligence in military contexts. It likely focuses on ensuring the security and accountability of military AI systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This act aims to address the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence. It may introduce regulations and guidelines to minimize AI's effects on the natural world.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from false or misleading AI-powered products and services. This act seeks to regulate AI technologies in a way that prioritizes consumer trust and protection.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from being misled by false or misleading information presented by artificial intelligence. This legislation likely focuses on regulating how AI interacts with the public to ensure transparency and honesty.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This Act aims to establish a system for heat emergency assessment and tracking using AI technology to provide early warnings and protect vulnerable populations. The law likely involves the development and implementation of AI-driven tools to monitor and respond to heat-related emergencies.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: The bill aims to create a central resource for biological research data access, enabling advanced computational methods like artificial intelligence. This resource would facilitate research by providing easier access to data.

title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives with respect to the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries.
action: Placed on the House Calendar, Calendar No. 67.
summary: The US House of Representatives has expressed its views on using artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries. The document aims to explore the potential benefits and implications of artificial intelligence in these sectors.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act aims to help small businesses advance in the area of artificial intelligence. The exact policies and details are not specified in the provided title and description.

title: A bill to require the Office of Financial Research to compel data relating to the financing of artificial intelligence development, provide that data to Congress, and issue recommendations to financial regulatory agencies and Congress to mitigate financial stability risk, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: This bill would require the Office of Financial Research to collect data on the financing of artificial intelligence development and report to Congress to assess financial stability risks. It also aims to recommend actions to Congress and financial regulatory agencies to mitigate these potential risks.

title: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
summary: The Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act aims to prevent the use of artificial intelligence in ways that deceive or mislead voters in elections. This legislation likely seeks to ensure the integrity of elections by mitigating the spread of AI-generated misinformation and propaganda.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a policy that likely deals with the regulation and use of artificial intelligence in agricultural settings. The specifics of the policy are not available, but it may address topics such as AI-powered farming tools and technology.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act aims to support and empower small businesses involved in AI innovation. Its exact details are unclear without an available description.

title: A bill to amend title 10, United States Code, to establish a policy for the Department of Defense on maximizing autonomy and artificial intelligence systems, to establish requirements relating to Department review and verification of autonomous weapon systems, and artificial intelligence capabilities, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: This bill aims to create a policy at the Department of Defense for maximizing the use of artificial intelligence and autonomous systems. It also establishes requirements for reviewing and verifying the capabilities and use of autonomous weapon systems.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a proposed law aimed at preventing the theft of artificial intelligence models in the US. Its purpose is to protect American AI innovations and prevent intellectual property theft related to AI technology.

title: NSF AI Education Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The NSF AI Education Act of 2025 is a policy that aims to promote education and training in artificial intelligence. It likely focuses on investing in programs and initiatives that support AI research and development.
